title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A platform reliability team. Release engineers need a Go artifact fetcher that admits only approved destinations, bounds compressed and decoded bytes, validates metadata, and proves the downloaded digest before promotion. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Artifact Fetch and Verification Agent with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Serialize intentional JSON and validate unknown decoded values before admitting them to trusted domain types. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.why: Retrieving Destination Validation through the concrete a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Destination Validation, keep Destination Validation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

hints: For Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Destination Validation, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Destination Validation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Destination Validation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: Go stakeholder SDK stores a response with max-age=50, ETag "rev-5", Age:5, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Destination Validation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A regular expression over the original URL text is sufficient destination security. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
